claimText: The Bhagirathi Eco Sensitive Zone was established to protect the stretch of the River Ganga between Gaumukh and Uttarkashi.
evidenceExcerpt: Created to protect the pristine stretch of the River Ganga between Gaumukh and Uttarkashi.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical extent and purpose of the Bhagirathi ESZ.
distractorHooks: Different rivers like Yamuna or Alaknanda
Different stretches like Devprayag to Haridwar

claimText: Eco-sensitive Zones are notified by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
evidenceExcerpt: Notified by MoEF&CC under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
examUse: Statement-based question on the statutory backing of Eco-sensitive Zones.

claimText: Eco-sensitive Zones act as 'shock absorbers' around Protected Areas and wildlife corridors.
evidenceExcerpt: Eco-sensitive Zones act as 'Shock Absorbers' around Protected Areas and wildlife corridors.
examUse: Conceptual statement-based question on the ecological role of ESZs.
distractorHooks: Zones where all human activity is completely banned

claimText: The management of the Bhagirathi Eco Sensitive Zone requires a Zonal Master Plan based on a watershed approach.
evidenceExcerpt: Requires a Zonal Master Plan (ZMP) based on a watershed approach.
examUse: Statement-based question on the management framework of the Bhagirathi ESZ.
distractorHooks: Based on an administrative boundary approach
Managed directly by the Central Water Commission
